fix: enable Tauri updater artifact generation

Tauri v2 does not create updater bundles (.app.tar.gz for macOS,
.nsis.zip for Windows) by default. The 'createUpdaterArtifacts'
option must be explicitly set. Without this, latest.json had an empty
platforms object and the updater could not find any matching platform.

feat: Menu flottant — verre givré + pill horizontale + drag (#76) (#85)

Aligne le menu flottant sur la spec visuelle §4.1 : remplacement du
dropdown vertical opaque par une pilule frosted glass avec expansion
horizontale et drag & drop.

## Objet

`#floating-menu` → `#ontowave-floating-menu` : refonte complète du menu
flottant selon spec §4.1 (verre givré, expansion horizontale, drag
souris+touch).

## Changements

**`src/main.ts`**
- `BOOTSTRAP_CSS` : CSS complet spec §4.1 injecté dynamiquement (compact
66×66px cercle → pilule 22px border-radius étendue)
- `bootstrapDom(cfg)` : crée `#ontowave-floating-menu` avec 🌊 icon,
brand OntoWave.org, 🏠 Accueil, boutons de langue depuis `cfg.roots`
- Drag & drop inline (mouse + touch), borné au viewport, session
uniquement
- Toggle expand/collapse sur l'icône, fermeture au clic extérieur

```ts
// État compact → pilule étendue
#ontowave-floating-menu:not(.expanded) { width: 66px; height: 66px; border-radius: 44px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.95); backdrop-filter: blur(10px); }
#ontowave-floating-menu.expanded       { border-radius: 22px; padding: 10px 18px; display: flex; gap: 10px; }
```

**`src/index.ts` · `src/adapters/browser/ux.ts`**
- Sélecteurs `#floating-menu` → `#ontowave-floating-menu` (dont print
CSS)

**`docs/index.html`**
- Div renommée, CSS remplacé par spec §4.1, surcharges thème dark/sepia
mises à jour

**`tests/e2e/demos/main-page.spec.js`**
- Sélecteur mis à jour + 2 nouveaux tests : expand/collapse (classe
`.expanded`, `aria-expanded`, visibilité brand) et contenu icône/bouton
🏠

## Checklist
- [x] Convention de commit respectée
- [x] Lint OK / Type-check OK / Tests OK
- [x] Docs/README mis à jour si nécessaire
- [x] Breaking changes documentées ou aucune
- [x] Tests ajoutés ou N/A
- [x] Portée petite et réversible

## Notes
- L'ancienne barre UX (thème/print/notes) s'injecte toujours dans
`#ontowave-floating-menu` via `ux.ts` — visible en état étendu après les
boutons de langue.
- Drag désactivé quand `.expanded` est présent ; position non persistée
(session uniquement, pas de `localStorage`).
- Breaking : tout HTML hôte utilisant `#floating-menu` doit être migré
vers `#ontowave-floating-menu`.

---------

fix: Unhardcode footer_vision_link: use relative path instead of absolute URL (#13178)

The `footer_vision_link` translation key was hardcoded to
`https://world.openfoodfacts.org/open-food-facts-vision-mission-values-and-programs`
across all 153 po files — unlike every other footer link (e.g.
`/who-we-are`, `/data`, `/press`) which uses a relative path. This broke
the translated-route matching in `Routing.pm` and locked the link to
`world.openfoodfacts.org` regardless of subdomain.

## Changes

- **`po/common/common.pot` + `po/common/en.po`**: Changed
`footer_vision_link` msgid/msgstr from the absolute URL to
`/open-food-facts-vision-mission-values-and-programs`
- **152 other `po/common/*.po` files**: Updated msgstr to relative path;
also fixed a typo in `ro.po` where the URL was missing the trailing `s`
- **67 integration test expected HTML files**: Updated rendered `href`
values to reflect the new relative path

```diff
-msgid "https://world.openfoodfacts.org/open-food-facts-vision-mission-values-and-programs"
-msgstr "https://world.openfoodfacts.org/open-food-facts-vision-mission-values-and-programs"
+msgid "/open-food-facts-vision-mission-values-and-programs"
+msgstr "/open-food-facts-vision-mission-values-and-programs"
```

The relative path is correctly handled by
`init_translated_text_routes_for_all_languages()` in `Texts.pm`, which
strips any leading slash before registering the slug as a route.

fix: align time estimation constants with training domain skill

Calibrate all constants against Neo's training-domain SKILL.md
(evidence-based exercise science reference):

- REST_TIME_STRENGTH: 180→240s (skill: 3-5 min, 4 min mid-range)
- REST_TIME_POWER: 240→300s (skill: full ATP-PC recovery for PL singles)
- REP_DURATION_COMPOUND: 4→5s (brace + eccentric + grind = ~5s/rep)
- TRANSITION_TIME: split into COMPOUND(150s) vs ISOLATION(60s)
  — loading plates on a barbell ≠ grabbing dumbbells
- Isolation rest: flat -30s → proportional 0.6× (prevents 210s curls
  in a strength session; yields ~144s str, ~54s hyp, realistic)
- Accessory rest: flat -45s → proportional 0.45× (120s str, 45s hyp)
- General fitness reps: 10-15 → 12-20 (skill: '12-20 reps, 2-3 sets')

Net effect: 60-min strength ≈ 4 exercises, 60-min hypertrophy ≈ 6-7,
60-min general fitness ≈ 8+. Feels like real gym sessions.

fix: use absolute paths for static resources in App.razor

Relative paths for _framework, _content, js, and favicon references

Fix date timezone issue and patient ID lookup in clinical notes

Fixed two critical bugs in backend/src/routes/notes.ts:

   - normalizeDateInput was using .toISOString() which converts to UTC
   - This caused dates to be off by 1 day depending on timezone
   - Fixed: Use local date components (getFullYear, getMonth, getDate)
   - Now: 'today' is stored as correct local date, not UTC date

2. Patient ID lookup bug:
   - POST endpoint receives patientId as string (e.g. 'P001')
   - But clinical_notes.patient_id is INTEGER in database

   - Now: Convert patient_id string to database integer ID before storing

- All date/time fields align correctly
- Note creation works without type errors

commit.message:
Terminate child processes on scan cancellation

Add cancellation-aware async shell methods (runAsync/shellAsync) that
use withTaskCancellationHandler to call process.terminate() when the
parent Swift Task is cancelled. This ensures gdu, clamscan, fclones,
czkawka, osqueryi, and brew subprocesses are killed immediately when
the user clicks Stop — not left running as orphans.

- ShellExecutor: Add runAsync()/shellAsync() with Process lifecycle

Implement quickCaptureService.ts for Loss Aversion mechanism (Engagement
Engine §机制11). Factory pattern with:

- capture(): create inspiration entities in KG
- listUnused(): query unarchived, unused inspirations sorted by decay
- markUsed(): link inspiration to chapter (idempotent)
- archiveStale(): auto-archive > 14-day inspirations
- getDecayStats(): tier counts (fresh/reminder/fading/archived)
- dispose(): cleanup

Follows exact patterns of foreshadowingTracker.ts / storyStatusService.ts:
  DbLikeWithRun interface, prepared statements, 30s TTL cache,
  injectable clock, assertNotDisposed guard, parameter validation.

49 tests with in-memory SQLite covering: CRUD, decay tiers, boundary
conditions, cache invalidation, disposal, parameter validation, mixed states.

Closes #144

* fix(kg,capture): R5 — type guard for dedicated-service entities + cache boundary post-filter

Duck R4 findings:
1. kgMutationSkill now rejects entity:create for 'inspiration'/'foreshadowing'
   types with INVALID_ARGUMENT — these must go through their dedicated services
   to avoid parseAttributes() mangling structured JSON attributes.

* fix(kg): R6 — guard entity:update patch.type for dedicated-service types

Duck R5 finding: entity:update payload carries patch.type (optional), which
allows converting a normal entity INTO inspiration/foreshadowing via generic
KG CRUD, reopening the parseAttributes() data corruption path.

Fix: kgMutationSkill validateMutation() now checks patch.type on entity:update
and rejects DEDICATED_SERVICE_ONLY_TYPES with INVALID_ARGUMENT.

Tests: +3 (inspiration/foreshadowing rejected, character allowed) → 2899 total

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(kgMutationSkill): runtime guard — reject entity:update on existing dedicated entities

Duck R6 found that even with patch.type blocked, a caller could still
entity:update an already-existing inspiration/foreshadowing entity by id,
overwriting attributes_json with Record<string,string> and corrupting
structured JSON (arrays/booleans).

Fix: execute() now calls entityRead() before entityUpdate() — if the
existing entity's type is in DEDICATED_SERVICE_ONLY_TYPES, returns
INVALID_ARGUMENT. 3 new tests cover inspiration, foreshadowing, and
the character-allowed positive case.

Tests: 154 files, 2902 tests, typecheck green.

* fix(kgMutationSkill): fail-closed — propagate entityRead errors before entityUpdate

Duck R7 found that the runtime guard fails open: if entityRead() returns
DB_ERROR or NOT_FOUND, the guard was bypassed and entityUpdate() still
executed, reopening the corruption path for dedicated entities.

entityUpdate if entityRead succeeded AND type is not dedicated.

2 new tests: DB_ERROR propagated, NOT_FOUND propagated — both assert
entityUpdate is NOT called.

commit.message:
DOC-371: Add traffic policy structure reference (#2048)

Users had no clear, single place to understand the overall shape of a
traffic policy document — which top-level keys exist, how rules nest
within phases, and how expressions and actions compose together.

## Changes

- **`traffic-policy/structure.mdx`** — Created a new standalone
documentation page providing a comprehensive YAML structure reference:
- Full annotated template covering all three phases (`on_tcp_connect`,
`on_http_request`, `on_http_response`) with inline comments explaining
each field
- Rule fields reference table documenting `name`, `expressions`, and
`actions` (required vs. optional)
- Complete multi-rule, multi-phase example demonstrating real-world
patterns in both YAML and JSON formats

- **`docs.json`** — Added the new structure page to the documentation
navigation under "Getting Started" (before the agent/cloud endpoint
guides) for easy discoverability

The new structure page addresses user feedback requesting a clear
reference similar to AWS CloudFormation, Terraform, and NGINX
documentation that shows the complete document structure at a glance.

```yaml
on_http_request:
  # Rule 1: block a specific IP address
  - name: "block bad actor"
    expressions:
      - "conn.client_ip == '1.2.3.4'"
    actions:
      - type: deny
        config:
          status_code: 403

  # Rule 2: require authentication on /admin paths
  - name: "admin oauth"
    expressions:
      - "req.url.path.startsWith('/admin')"
    actions:
      - type: oauth
        config:
          provider: google

  # Rule 3: no expressions — forward all remaining traffic
  - actions:
      - type: forward-internal
        config:
          url: https://example.internal

on_http_response:
  - actions:
      - type: add-headers
        config:
          headers:
            x-frame-options: "DENY"
```
